chief_complaint: Pleuritic chest pain with multiple VTE risk factors — high suspicion for pulmonary embolism
clinical_reasoning: PATTERN RECOGNITION: 28-year-old female on drospirenone-containing OCP (highest VTE risk class among combined oral contraceptives, OR 6.3 vs non-use) for 14 months, PLUS 12-hour car drive 5 days ago (prolonged immobilization exceeding 4 hours is an independent VTE risk factor), PLUS family history of DVT in paternal aunt at age 45 (suggests possible inherited thrombophilia such as Factor V Leiden or prothrombin gene mutation), PLUS prior ED visit 3 weeks ago for RIGHT CALF PAIN diagnosed as 'muscle strain' WITHOUT Doppler ultrasound performed — this is a CLASSIC presentation of a MISSED DVT that has now progressed to pulmonary embolism. Today's findings: SpO2 95% is ABNORMAL for a healthy 28-year-old (expected baseline 98-100%, representing a 3-5% drop from normal). HR 102 is NOT anxiety — it is compensatory tachycardia from V/Q mismatch and increased dead-space ventilation. Low-grade fever 37.2°C is consistent with pulmonary infarction. Pain reproducible on palpation does NOT exclude PE — published evidence shows chest wall tenderness in 19.9% of PE patients vs 23.8% of non-PE patients (p=0.25, no significant difference). The multiplicative VTE risk from OCP + immobilization + family history exceeds 30× baseline. ORANGE — very urgent, physician assessment within 20 minutes, STAT imaging.
vital_sign_concerns: SpO2 95% — ABNORMAL for a 28-year-old (expected 98-100%), represents significant A-a gradient widening
HR 102 — compensatory tachycardia from V/Q mismatch, NOT anxiety
risk_factors: Drospirenone OCP × 14 months (highest VTE risk class, OR 6.3 vs non-use)
Family history DVT — paternal aunt at age 45 (suggests inherited thrombophilia)
Prior ED visit 3 weeks ago: right calf pain diagnosed as muscle strain WITHOUT Doppler
dont_miss: Saddle embolus with right ventricular strain
